About me

A little background

I'm a final-year Electronics & Communication Engineering student at VIT-AP University, graduating in 2026. My path has taken me through hardware fundamentals, data analytics, and frontend development — and I've found my sweet spot where code meets communication.

I enjoy building React applications that feel intuitive, writing technical content that actually makes sense to readers, and exploring cloud platforms. I believe the best work happens when engineering rigour meets good design instinct.

Outside tech, I've won a university-level NEP 2020 Essay Competition, written a bilingual English-Telugu short story, and have a deep interest in cinema and filmmaking.

Selected work

Things I've built

🌤️

Frontend App

TripWeather

A full React app combining real-time weather (Open-Meteo), an interactive Leaflet.js map, reverse geocoding via Nominatim, and place suggestions from OpenTripMap. Deployed on Vercel via GitHub CI.

ReactLeaflet.jsOpen-MeteoVercel
Live demo ↗ GitHub ↗
⚙️

Full-Stack Assessment

Pipeline Builder

A React + FastAPI pipeline builder with DAG cycle detection, dark-themed UI, and node-based visual graph editor. Built as a technical assessment for VectorShift — discovered via Y Combinator hiring platform.

ReactFastAPIDAGPython
GitHub ↗
📡

ECE Research Project

BER Simulation

Noise-induced error analysis in digital communication systems, studying BPSK, QPSK, 8-PSK, 16-QAM, and 64-QAM under AWGN with Hamming (7,4) error correction. Includes an interactive HTML simulator.

MATLABSimulinkHTMLSignal Processing
GitHub ↗
